preamble: The shift from a velar-lateral affricate [kʟ] to an alveolar fricative [s] represents a simplification and assibilation of a complex dorsal-lateral sound into a common sibilant fricative.

examples.note: Complex lateral affricates can undergo fronting and simplification into more common sibilants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
